steps.title: Identify the grammatical structure
steps.content: The subject noun phrase is “Calida Garcia Rawles,” which follows the introductory participial phrase. On the right of the blank is the main verb “was,” which begins the predicate. There is no intervening phrase or clause between them.

steps.title: Apply the punctuation rule
steps.content:
No punctuation belongs between a subject and its main verb. The em dash (A), colon (B), and comma (C) all incorrectly separate them.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
